title: hypericum coris
intro: Hypericum coris, also known as the bugleweed or robin-run-the-hedge, is a perennial herb in the St. John's Wort family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other compounds that provide numerous health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Hypericum coris is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
